clean_body: | ~~ This month, Gov. Ron DeSantis’ 6-week abortion ban went into effect in Florida – a near total ban that destroys the right to bodily autonomy for millions of people. Make no mistake, Peter: This is forced birth, and a fascist attack on Floridian’s rights and health. But we have a chance to reverse it. Amendment 4 would enshrine the right to abortion in Florida’s constitution, and it’s on the ballot this November. This is our best opportunity to end this ban and put healthcare decisions back in the hands of Floridians — where they belong. That’s why my campaign has been on the ground alongside Floridians Protecting Freedom, organizing and mobilizing voters to pass Amendment 4. But we need your help to ramp up our organizing on the ground and reach more voters before anti-choice extremists bombard them with misinformation. ~~ | | | ~~ ~~ | ~~ ~ | ~ ~ Fighting together, Maxwell Frost ~~ ~ | PAID FOR BY MAXWELL FROST FOR CONGRESS Maxwell Frost for Congress PO Box 772671 Orlando, FL 32877-2671 United States | Maxwell is an organizer representing Florida's 10th Congressional District in Congress. name: Working Families Party
subject: The May WFP Newsletter!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St
body:
Read on to learn what we’ve been up to in the last month, and how you can take action to keep up the fight. | | | We’re officially in the _thick_ of election season, Peter — and we’re continuing to organize on the ground to deliver victories for working people. Think you missed any WFP updates from last month? Don’t sweat it! **Here’s your WFP newsletter to catch you up:**
* * *
**_REP. SUMMER LEE OVERCOMES ATTACKS FROM THREE BILLIONAIRE-BACKED SUPER PACS_** This past month, we celebrated Rep. Summer Lee’s primary victory in Pennsylvania’s 12th Congressional District. Voters delivered a _massive_ blow to the right-wing Super PACs who funneled swaths of cash from some of the wealthiest billionaires in the country to pull out every single trick in the book and take her down. They strongly said **NO** to the right-wing agenda that wants to defund our schools, bust our unions, pollute our planet, and destroy our futures. [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9fJdgyodWMPcl758EV5KKZI8foHUbm2Y2AM9a0zdl7Mwjj3NsH0yipVjkOmZu0t4xGhpxZhKDv-2oV_NiiU6GGb4MYjjTiJHxtNcQNSnr3S2zb__0nhi1LKoRhIk79LnIuoZdxw6haZD-BjOr9AC_Jqaxyt7lwi70z_WlcQikcK9jLVnFzEEcsf8OxCNkmNTK0xTjvBIWz3Ng94bqHwuCR9fbSpVRNQ3WsxZOti6tjjJKBhMd84qF8TS-TRnPlwOIhTkMK5RTZiV5hQM2B5dP_bs4Lb0vxKVGJf5sG4y20JI-yHGDk1pzFn5Hycrt3oPLp2KCCd0CzDMKXtGXWeb0Unsi7YUeBDIWez7d1wKc6oU/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h0/h001.HEHxP80RyQGerBrQGPNcjRX37J9qLhzTDcqEMaT-G7E) This 20-point victory could not have been possible without the hard work of WFP members and volunteers who made over 50,000 calls, sent out nearly 500,000 texts, and delivered more than 6,000 postcards to voters in Summer’s district. Together, we showed the right-wing lobby that their millions are no match for our people-powered movement, and guaranteed one of the biggest champions for working people in the country another two years in Congress — where she’ll continue fighting for our priorities. For coverage of the commanding victory — and the role of the WFP and allies in securing it — see [**The Guardian**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMRXuNSlhDxcvv3N1sDz0loluKN7sdZN-7orPDNziziQO6WRAXDji02c7V-VEkRRD6T5gPN9OsOy4rpGFV1Kltnl4JqvXuaWqod6whrXfW9xLJRXn6dq0s7QmX6ZIq7B4uPyJYN48_t3LZhgLgTUM7MsCNTBzxSIXlf-Ejbxq9lLgiG4akGOFYK3-IzXrMZ_XGqIysw2YDJSYzvdQKySFHD6h8WW4v5LDYJZom5F_ls3C0C0a7pb3Wt0lCZIh9te8MLWq3a7rgY-xkdSRDPHGuZhbSdlLvhUqDdto4657u-m1dboiX5WxFI6R3oEenSbkzyHgpZkQOkfWdhf3_09DUzwL4If0ChMof4UY8KKG2f7I/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h1/h001.e823Qze4ubLiTpOMHlbqhDVg3n-0Gst3W3xRYFib2EI), [**CNN**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMTHpY68KeBL-tuPFtNUwAZSDHJnWN6M8ju-rtAxbSW7Vc-sdQwUEnMbb_UMfMliWysVdS-TjDWUlS45El6V5YNZk_OdGO8RL9sZHcgRk9zLw7zMrarM8_Shd-RfvVQm71QKTLAetT4SJ_e854bjIYwzgLxytbVMSqEZAsOjNsaOT2sNGtPNDVo5YW4oFnVH0_VgCZaQIv0QWR6kbrdIaYOqiDnnTPt0GnWfi2phohrJ7pf7Lv8jmlFdg5cPSzZ6qL7zK5ZN3cI1QbZ9tld-3b6F6glEjzF2tBj3hjYHtAU2n6sVJM3Qp15vJZlYE-RbSc0MeFR1npK6niZLYqGyzE-8PvPcuJZaK5q5X0KslRyrX/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h2/h001._WdAqnEQCXMhuuQ061ACv2auQa62aQ8j5Ox1Fg2_PXo), [ **The Intercept**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.SEgofQhSb9pGg8bz5XQG3dunYsGAwMwtOP6MfWrSotRAIb2BLmPfnqg6No-qxX4JAdB_bpaiWNYYmWa0tu6EHiClQ3goChzZYAHJHyjrlypPkFP9xRheg92nDjnA7AObqSb-p023Z7-HxsW39ADa_TW2An2WjtouPXZjNcFyaeq3y1evUAi9SCy1IJ0slrnXhLIhk0Q4vMnXiswX66SXoN4-_6ZQTqCOK9kkqXFRvcQlUFTsE0bYp5ZfWKJSPbMU_1-_p4dSNy0DBHtO00lHth-E0RXkYQuGBvJCdvugRf9vfwzYICZ57AA05gt6y_-OaX02ZseQZagBDUOLOiA_1Q/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h3/h001.XtygjegkiJzrYGWPteoql9EbIiXk8qh0zQEyKaT0o5w), and [**The Nation**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMcW_8R5UJsK9uMVIoldEBjkJy9of_FdAXkZYFjJ12QTQyWX_YN6XReEhUmWOHemuz5cNxkoW05U8oYBdMHXRHZ6liRW0900gJviKqG9llcXdr9Npua0xjO9ZugfEJG_MgKIOxP7boY4QANEaysA4aJsNE4EHATdRhooiY4sXSfPyxn1-Rbi9nl3q0zAEfGUWow8GfXCBW7rFRRMuKTI3Tx2JU_ofaCoGQmDzF85gyIRnQ5ymf82Mx5x0Qh_M_lIMutrPHhJXuGWn0irBJWlbYehoyy69sEQLgM6mccsoS7FMHzJ-t3nLWYQOo5m8NssdW0zDcEqd1luK3hcATrCJPf4/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h4/h001.ON6ijXTMP1DcQQYa1uKSr-SYq7q7ce8KKRI3a3Wm2gE).
* * *
**_DEFENDING RIGHT-WING SUPER PACS’ NEXT TARGET: JAMAAL BOWMAN_**
This month, we’re turning our attention to our other champions in Congress who are under threat, starting with **Jamaal Bowman** in New York whose primary is on June 25th. Jamaal’s facing a tough primary challenge from AIPAC, who’s far-right megadonors are expected to spend up to $25 million to attack him and prop up his opponent, George Latimer — **which could make this the most expensive House primary in U.S. history.** With _this_ much spending against Jamaal, and with recent polls showing this race is neck-and-neck — we need your help making calls and speaking with the voters in these critical final weeks [**RSVP now for our phonebank with Jamaal Bowman this Monday, May 13th at 5:30PM ET/2:30PM PT, and help make a difference in this close race — or scroll down for more volunteer opportunities in the weeks ahead.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxbgnOOkdYqkjQ_-4XfSIiSWK_RPOnu7qBnwfh9rPnxe07xtDejw_N1Vf4ZQU-6F9EZTSGLTGZXn2HVim3A1Md2bpCTtI4UIANuBkFaIjaHvE9glOW1rSZ3FsBiqauKze-d0SYgEn_-Kez-gDJyhe69V9eGVJIht3WzX44GriQ1n8aVlPaK6IljzXCIpLiWih6p5GXaky7yviWsiKW9bWNnw/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h5/h001.ptUTZpdOs-LALyet92nKLtzqUTUWLxoDEDSN1nqfF1I) **[RSVP: Bowman Phone Bank »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxbgnOOkdYqkjQ_-4XfSIiSWK_RPOnu7qBnwfh9rPnxe07xtDejw_N1Vf4ZQU-6F9EBZ8HjnkdGe0rl3hHWU5fFcscVwBPLrSpG323zwDT8lK1AqXG1C00KJN6J2ma38xRz2jRXoXWodqJvgPANxttaTPoVuxZ3qhqiQvsYuotp9Mw3zM8i8VoIXQ2rEZ7FvVqMuCc93JxFah40_0t6cM_aA/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h6/h001.7puJLYKdVzRia4sApFOwCdhiNApHHnwj77EBMWsTwPo)** **__**
* * *
**_MORE ELECTION RESULTS FROM PENNSYLVANIA_**
In addition to Rep. Summer Lee’s victory, we celebrated some other major primary wins in Pennsylvania. State Rep. **Malcolm Kenyatta** — who we supported in the 2022 Pennsylvania U.S. Senate race — won his primary for Pennsylvania Auditor General by 30 points, and will advance to the general election against the incumbent Republican auditor. WFP champion **Andre Carroll** also cruised to victory in the race for a Germantown-based State House seat in Philadelphia. If he wins again in November, he’ll be the district’s first openly gay representative and one of a handful of Black LGBTQ+ members in the Pennsylvania State Legislature. Lehigh Valley’s **Anna Thomas** and Lancaster County’s **Brad Chambers** also won their State House primaries and will challenge incumbent Republican representatives in November’s general election. Other WFP challengers who ran for state office, like **Keir Bradford Grey** , WFP’s endorsed candidate in the Pennsylvania Attorney General race, and **Ashley Comans** in Pennsylvania’s District 34 State House race, unfortunately came up short — as did **Cass Green** in her rematch against of the most conservative Democrats in the Pennsylvania House, losing by just **42 votes** after a recount. Still, we are so incredibly proud of the campaigns each of these candidates ran, and we know that the future of Pennsylvania is brighter for it. And finally, we racked up several wins for WFP-endorsed incumbents, including State Sens. **Nikil Saval** (SD-1) and **Amanda Cappelletti** (SD-17), and State Reps. **Izzy Smith Wade-El** (HD-49), **Carol Kazeem** (HD-159), **Elizabeth Fiedler** (HD-184), **Rick Krajewski** (HD-188), **Roni Green** (HD-190), **Tarik Khan** (HD-194), **Chris Rabb** (HD-200). **__**
* * *
**_OUR RESPONSE TO VIOLENT ATTACKS ON GAZA SOLIDARITY STUDENT ENCAMPMENTS_**
Since October 7th, more than 35,000 Palestinians have been killed in an indiscriminate bombing campaign that the U.S. continues to fund with no end in sight — and across the country, students are boldly rising up, demanding that their universities divest from Israeli companies complicit in the war on Gaza. **Following the violent, militarized response from on-campus and state law enforcement against the student encampments for Palestine on college campuses,** we expressed our solidarity with students calling for the preservation of human life and an end to the devastation in Gaza, condemned the brutal crackdown on protestors, and reaffirmed our determination to continue the fight to end unconditional military aid to Israel and establish a lasting ceasefire. [**You can read our full statement here.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.SEgofQhSb9pGg8bz5XQG3TwLUsxlG3Mgk3OKCFfwvH276kWZS-TtXLuvmzptYwvof8hKVqFr0Pwwi5ECe9W4xUlV2l3ISwB4nI43vKs8yII4JTf31BjiEMJYrLVaqleF966DUeQYhssGXZbOONPYxiF_Hhn4vjMCilSjtO0LnOWluhqm9GpIl65mKlOOvBMxdeealveYFgyy5heWPEPtFoQqQ5vkiyCmP7TSOnYC7Rzs6dDSF44IDdpDBCVkdM1L7unlQmtwtLxLazYrZOFKgAuvVJIkVyalZ_9iPYWYnCTUZukD71_IKv11DsT-9Oo-km_ep_HEzvj3jArNZe_9_g/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h7/h001.1zfV2s5gEKckSQuR35hmos3BrKkc2IcO7ETr4rsREJ0)
* * *
**_CONNECTICUT WORKING FAMILIES PARTY HELPS PASS MAJOR EXPANSION OF PAID SICK LEAVE LAW_**
This past Monday, the Connecticut State Senate passed **H.B. 5005** , expanding paid sick days to more workers across the state. The [**CT Monitor**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.EtNV8HBC60Tl7UuGmXS3sdUXx0mBVRJD3rlMHGfiwc4U2U_qzQz151fWd4YqDL7ZEt55UzYwocfbxJUiYmloig_EQLOfB6Eu9pjUa3-jUF9_2nwcRYLnaQ8JNUW2UPGCZKbJZ8jOCuTDr1fZJYQzMiUyVIA6Vo_vXchR9J_FHkcIjaAIzY321oIBbPizLv2RLKIIGLOy09VkvbYQzbCjymGZtHChFcTG9lCA-GhBk0Cq8IBVlqwXtsDKNg2gzuL6IWH7Bw2gRs4JEJ1_GG6SbB78gshhbIDv_IXRFkAOR3Zf93YGOP-bODVtvD6AVQuyci3SVCjTqo024-LVwWsP7A/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h8/h001.dtGlbieFLurmTcUeiRv6g1hGs6iBe5uFXGji-fFd-2c) called it “a victory for the Working Families Party,” after we led the lobbying effort for the bill's passage. From paid family and medical leave to a $15 minimum wage, Connecticut WFP has been _the_ driving force behind several of the biggest victories for working people in the Nutmeg State. **But this month’s victory wouldn't have been possible without the tireless efforts of advocates and volunteers across the state — and the work of CTWFP activists, leaders, and legislative champions.**
* * *
**_NEW ENDORSEMENTS IN DELAWARE, NEW MEXICO, CALIFORNIA, AND KANSAS_** In **Delaware** , we announced our first round of endorsements for the 2024 election season. [**Click here to see who we’re supporting.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYd6eLP33O2GRZpvuK-wGuTY14OnJzgHzuEj-1B0JUZUn0SbPBOwAm2bVqb_P95kIpGjO3U2v5_CEofhinaHMMfxXDH9vBBbc_DBfEajZnzIv4hvNNH-9qwO1sgqH8kIEGjqi-QvQRes-gXjg1djXQGxzpU9vlUJYVCJqLCWuHBBQQu-RVO4byqarl-C_lZhu07e63wnX_pbChfvxbZDNjg11JP_pUFK_eqNP4Q_egWFgWRZprKa8-sOUVzGQIDCFq3GP2_cWRnl-7IJ21w6hb65gKstbOUAB_foUnBXlIbz/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h9/h001.OUKBlhUTdMoO2EV3K5nzCDizBiIuhMc4NdK13awIdoA) Over the last two election cycles, the Delaware Working Families Party has endorsed 12 state legislative candidates and has won in 7 of those races. In 2024, we’re seeking to repeat those successes and elect leaders who will move us forward on critical issues that working-class people in Delaware want progress on, like free school meals for all, true police reform, and community workforce agreements. To read more on our endorsement of Collin O’Mara for Delaware Governor and first round of House seats, check out the [**Delaware News-Journal**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYcU620jwk-g7Ee82ngEejGF6jpg5Ga-8mBTBWpVWhgUq8CIVSjstBnJaF-4nhM1ZzOVEgw4IZ9VPgCwrTPnW0KhI08PJEAC6S634YpeezUNMLPF2sRrUJEybWk0u2SKG7I9mL4Mh4Eg_hVoKn7QGOw9-ldi1s4aMc2JZaYI6TQXOn3KKy_dFqj-Dsq245L2G8e5X3lJgOn07wpsYKI9MFxRQUZYe-2QxNf-6VCwhCU0Od1J6IBVHJ_Wk2AmXGJCYIV_hnSUqsdouCuSIm5g3WHoUMHb0uxoKcscrSImbuZbVCk6-sCNxnnGYP63lknUIt_fJDszYaGmkF4MJldx6IgnFy7U2qfwytLNhJiZddA3JnoxlEp3wbWXul4FMkvBRQObKHX5hkufrOe-4ilycghoB4iL4EzOKyYaQYQ2JBf2/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h10/h001.y--permgXgvmEJq5aP-eCh6VdCRi4P4vESVKCbaNZNU) and [**Delaware Liberal**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW2amWCk7GVMj2SaffSaoQ0y1GGWFQ2kOFOEnBJ2hFHkBqCJScHues61feCuWL4ZJ2VvqV_gpgCMG5qdGwZGGqZvQqX2Fk0kLjVEiQTgucnFR5SJdZxM1Esn429Q5eZCYSZZwk3LzByfDVdbqqFvoASCBy82D8Gc9IEGh0kKR3W-NYHqEt3f-DivhqLbNCTpKtUlLltz-pboXFAMD7z11DEudNZug8-wYsHKfPqeRQshQi1yghf3R1GaL7oB44-cjwkYkv0RCpgJc16ahlzlfpTw5eizi1TiFNdE_KUfc8CW8thhWqMPF2C48PkasPRrp98f_NbMVjB8u0XfHDHdztT6iYHQc2Siw8VgEqvHTJrwyWBH0yw6IoC4kIhXETWx8fw/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h11/h001.1Cpd8OpaflaCZT02n6M4skZsGlySC6L5sv54UF-YomI). Likewise in **New Mexico** , we rolled out our first round of endorsements for state and county offices ahead of the June primary election. [**You can see the full list of candidates here.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYd6eLP33O2GRZpvuK-wGuRR3YgyT-UfpKtZQnteUpY-gzHoE2fXBMg81qeviGelT7VCOsle2mnLMjxpWAZ-Szp3CiK9SDaSucuLgwfNMjYBqPRSsYEZrpTq0010GNeh30gegys1kNBaeG7tjcpo1xZbcJ7WjBIrmqi7QFBIMuyJWBd9dLiS7mlAtLVrFSJsvqwSydf3GZ0F7k-2EnnwrpRhZbRz67Fr_WJRaQGF1KEryH3MVHGq6s_I3hKh29mMyGejL4-JL1luoGlSoIdZKehfVFVwrF_zPxJUAPLS9VmW/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h12/h001.EqIioJ7cUinXjUXUBSK9jQDP-8uYSZiH01Wr3VYt6F0) New Mexico Working Families Party State Director JD Mathews noted, “We are incredibly proud to endorse this first round of candidates from across New Mexico. They are proven champions on the side of New Mexicans and not wealthy and well connected, big corporate interests. Local elections are at the center of investing in the needs of our communities and the leaders we have endorsed today understand the importance of improving our quality of life.” Meanwhile in **California** , we endorsed two candidates for Los Angeles City Council — **Ysabel Jurado** in District 14 and **Jillian Burgos** in District 2. [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYd6eLP33O2GRZpvuK-wGuSU3wypp6Z0bVTHbVlYfH3UVk0oIsYoFi2CkscCuwmZaGj43XHX-dOZzoDWaJkAxsSNn-nwlmVkTlfi02KInVjBcCsHDUMJPGbWl5XjHn7UxARGhShLRJ6AcbmKXQ5_BomjfATsO8p-yZX70Vfta5cLSZ4vnyeDh7scu2sduAtA59RpyEf16-aifdiwU5NiVsUtLtCT77HyyHzpLhfkwr_ifPLsuQgmGQv_SvigvVM1VQ/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h13/h001.jttwp9KAlkKKhY_NgleMA0trcxjZhkTjNMeTF2gBfLE) Los Angeles has proven to be a bright spot for California’s progressive movement, flexing its muscle at City Hall — and we’re excited to continue growing and building that strength. Last but not least, in **Kansas** , WFP has endorsed **Zach Thomas** for Johnson County District Attorney and **Byron Roberson** for Johnson County Sheriff. These two elections are our next opportunity to advance a fair and equitable criminal justice agenda, and elect more candidates that want to end mass incarceration, decriminalize poverty, and put our communities first. It’s also our latest effort to make further inroads in the midwest.
* * *
**_UPCOMING EVENTS:_**
[**Phone Bank for Jamaal Bowman!**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxbgnOOkdYqkjQ_-4XfSIiSWK_RPOnu7qBnwfh9rPnxe07xtDejw_N1Vf4ZQU-6F9EGMGqpVlRP-WseaTKP_ltB9vsjf5MRmnxHcGr5KlwuGpKHWVbkbKLHaTgNNNsvEENCsSdEQtxJBldYDiSSAf5eWOzECSXiPOCvnm7wHa6QYmLSlsfSsc_0hOI1GiFmaIpKawQa4PAc7YlPo79zxkS_w/469/iXYcUOtORV2JKopaJjBwJg/h14/h001.To_eYaosuZ6wfyTOxwCvebgFYbmF1_-4_mtiK22BhLc) **This Monday, join Rep. Jamaal Bowman at a virtual phone bank to support his re-election campaign!** Every Monday from now until the election, volunteers from across the country are making calls and speaking with the voters who will be deciding this race. Never made calls before? Don't worry, you'll get all of the training you need to call with confidence. All you need is a computer or internet-connected device and a phone. clean_body: Read on to learn what we’ve been up to in the last month, and how you can take action to keep up the fight. | | | We’re officially in the thick of election season, Peter — and we’re continuing to organize on the ground to deliver victories for working people. Think you missed any WFP updates from last month? Don’t sweat it! Here’s your WFP newsletter to catch you up: REP. SUMMER LEE OVERCOMES ATTACKS FROM THREE BILLIONAIRE-BACKED SUPER PACS This past month, we celebrated Rep. Summer Lee’s primary victory in Pennsylvania’s 12th Congressional District. Voters delivered a massive blow to the right-wing Super PACs who funneled swaths of cash from some of the wealthiest billionaires in the country to pull out every single trick in the book and take her down. They strongly said NO to the right-wing agenda that wants to defund our schools, bust our unions, pollute our planet, and destroy our futures. This 20-point victory could not have been possible without the hard work of WFP members and volunteers who made over 50,000 calls, sent out nearly 500,000 texts, and delivered more than 6,000 postcards to voters in Summer’s district. Together, we showed the right-wing lobby that their millions are no match for our people-powered movement, and guaranteed one of the biggest champions for working people in the country another two years in Congress — where she’ll continue fighting for our priorities. For coverage of the commanding victory — and the role of the WFP and allies in securing it — see , , , and . DEFENDING RIGHT-WING SUPER PACS’ NEXT TARGET: JAMAAL BOWMAN This month, we’re turning our attention to our other champions in Congress who are under threat, starting with Jamaal Bowman in New York whose primary is on June 25th. Jamaal’s facing a tough primary challenge from AIPAC, who’s far-right megadonors are expected to spend up to $25 million to attack him and prop up his opponent, George Latimer — which could make this the most expensive House primary in U.S. history. With this much spending against Jamaal, and with recent polls showing this race is neck-and-neck — we need your help making calls and speaking with the voters in these critical final weeks MORE ELECTION RESULTS FROM PENNSYLVANIA In addition to Rep. Summer Lee’s victory, we celebrated some other major primary wins in Pennsylvania. State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta — who we supported in the 2022 Pennsylvania U.S. Senate race — won his primary for Pennsylvania Auditor General by 30 points, and will advance to the general election against the incumbent Republican auditor. WFP champion Andre Carroll also cruised to victory in the race for a Germantown-based State House seat in Philadelphia. If he wins again in November, he’ll be the district’s first openly gay representative and one of a handful of Black LGBTQ+ members in the Pennsylvania State Legislature. Lehigh Valley’s Anna Thomas and Lancaster County’s Brad Chambers also won their State House primaries and will challenge incumbent Republican representatives in November’s general election. Other WFP challengers who ran for state office, like Keir Bradford Grey , WFP’s endorsed candidate in the Pennsylvania Attorney General race, and Ashley Comans in Pennsylvania’s District 34 State House race, unfortunately came up short — as did Cass Green in her rematch against of the most conservative Democrats in the Pennsylvania House, losing by just 42 votes after a recount. Still, we are so incredibly proud of the campaigns each of these candidates ran, and we know that the future of Pennsylvania is brighter for it. And finally, we racked up several wins for WFP-endorsed incumbents, including State Sens. Nikil Saval (SD-1) and Amanda Cappelletti (SD-17), and State Reps. Izzy Smith Wade-El (HD-49), Carol Kazeem (HD-159), Elizabeth Fiedler (HD-184), Rick Krajewski (HD-188), Roni Green (HD-190), Tarik Khan (HD-194), Chris Rabb (HD-200). OUR RESPONSE TO VIOLENT ATTACKS ON GAZA SOLIDARITY STUDENT ENCAMPMENTS Since October 7th, more than 35,000 Palestinians have been killed in an indiscriminate bombing campaign that the U.S. continues to fund with no end in sight — and across the country, students are boldly rising up, demanding that their universities divest from Israeli companies complicit in the war on Gaza. Following the violent, militarized response from on-campus and state law enforcement against the student encampments for Palestine on college campuses, we expressed our solidarity with students calling for the preservation of human life and an end to the devastation in Gaza, condemned the brutal crackdown on protestors, and reaffirmed our determination to continue the fight to end unconditional military aid to Israel and establish a lasting ceasefire. CONNECTICUT WORKING FAMILIES PARTY HELPS PASS MAJOR EXPANSION OF PAID SICK LEAVE LAW This past Monday, the Connecticut State Senate passed H.B. 5005 , expanding paid sick days to more workers across the state. The called it “a victory for the Working Families Party,” after we led the lobbying effort for the bill's passage. From paid family and medical leave to a $15 minimum wage, Connecticut WFP has been the driving force behind several of the biggest victories for working people in the Nutmeg State. But this month’s victory wouldn't have been possible without the tireless efforts of advocates and volunteers across the state — and the work of CTWFP activists, leaders, and legislative champions. NEW ENDORSEMENTS IN DELAWARE, NEW MEXICO, CALIFORNIA, AND KANSAS In Delaware , we announced our first round of endorsements for the 2024 election season. Over the last two election cycles, the Delaware Working Families Party has endorsed 12 state legislative candidates and has won in 7 of those races. In 2024, we’re seeking to repeat those successes and elect leaders who will move us forward on critical issues that working-class people in Delaware want progress on, like free school meals for all, true police reform, and community workforce agreements. To read more on our endorsement of Collin O’Mara for Delaware Governor and first round of House seats, check out the and . Likewise in New Mexico , we rolled out our first round of endorsements for state and county offices ahead of the June primary election. New Mexico Working Families Party State Director JD Mathews noted, “We are incredibly proud to endorse this first round of candidates from across New Mexico. They are proven champions on the side of New Mexicans and not wealthy and well connected, big corporate interests. Local elections are at the center of investing in the needs of our communities and the leaders we have endorsed today understand the importance of improving our quality of life.” Meanwhile in California , we endorsed two candidates for Los Angeles City Council — Ysabel Jurado in District 14 and Jillian Burgos in District 2. Los Angeles has proven to be a bright spot for California’s progressive movement, flexing its muscle at City Hall — and we’re excited to continue growing and building that strength. Last but not least, in Kansas , WFP has endorsed Zach Thomas for Johnson County District Attorney and Byron Roberson for Johnson County Sheriff. These two elections are our next opportunity to advance a fair and equitable criminal justice agenda, and elect more candidates that want to end mass incarceration, decriminalize poverty, and put our communities first. It’s also our latest effort to make further inroads in the midwest. UPCOMING EVENTS: This Monday, join Rep. Jamaal Bowman at a virtual phone bank to support his re-election campaign! Every Monday from now until the election, volunteers from across the country are making calls and speaking with the voters who will be deciding this race. Never made calls before? Don't worry, you'll get all of the training you need to call with confidence. All you need is a computer or internet-connected device and a phone. Help support our candidates at a WFP virtual text party next Tuesday, May 14 at 6PM ET/3PM PT! Our organizers and volunteer leaders will support you as you use our easy program to text voters on behalf of WFP champions across the country. This month, we’ll be texting to defend Squad Rep. Jamaal Bowman! On Thursday, May 23 at 7PM ET/4PM PT, join us for our monthly Welcome Gathering! These monthly calls are your opportunity — whether you’re new to the WFP or a long-time supporter — to hear directly from our staff and activists on the country and the party we’re building together. Thanks for making it this far, Peter! In solidarity, Working Families Party Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Working Families, please .

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 5/11/2024
body:
Your daily rundown of REAL news for REAL Coloradans.
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Happy Saturday, friends!
This is the Mountain Minute for Saturday, May 11, 2024.
- Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
| | **TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
---
**['Devastated' details the deadly fentanyl epidemic and calls Coloradans to action](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY BRIAN PORTER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Overdoses believed to be driven largely by fentanyl-laced prescription drugs are setting records annually.
The death count once was similar to the [capacity of a high school classroom](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) each week, marking the third largest cause of pediatric deaths behind firearm-related injuries and motor vehicle collisions, a report by UCLA Health reads. It has now grown beyond that analogy.
The drug often kills those [experimenting with it for the first time](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), with the Centers for Disease Control finding 86 percent of those adolescents dying of a fentanyl overdose had not previously experienced a drug overdose, according to a report in The Free Press.
Introduction of Fentanyl has been especially devastating in Colorado, where the Department of Public Health found in 2021 there were more than 900 deaths, and from 2020 to 2021 there was a 70 percent uptick in fentanyl deaths, [according to a CBS News report](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
An effort to share personal stories of tragedy related to fentanyl use and seek solutions will hit theaters this coming week. The film "[DEVASTATED: Colorado's Fentanyl Disaster](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)" will premiere in four select theaters from May 15-17.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[From bag fees to car fees, two ballot measures could end Colorado's fee-frenzy](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [CBS COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Colorado legislators have relied heavily on imposing fees, like a new [$5 daily fee on car rentals](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to fund mass transit, rather than raising taxes.
* The American Car Rental Association has [threatened to sue Colorado](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), claiming the fee violates federal law against non-airport benefiting fees. The rental car industry supports [two initiatives](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) that would restrict fee increases by requiring voter approval and linking fees directly to benefits received by those who pay them.
* The proposed rental car fee [risks federal funding](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) for Denver International Airport and increases consumer costs, with rental car fees constituting a significant portion of rental expenses.
* Colorado officials like Gov. Polis [defend the fee approach](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), while the rental car industry argues it threatens state financial stability and highlights its significant local economic contributions, including employment and tax revenue.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[The Most Legit Dude' in the Colorado Capitol: Senator becomes unlikely celebrity](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [WESTWORD](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Colorado [State Senator Perry Will](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) has become an iconic figure at the Capitol, celebrated for his distinctive style featuring wolf fur and bolo ties, and his unique approach to politics that includes challenging Denver's mayor to a quirky eating contest.
* The senator's popularity has [sparked "Perry Will Friday,"](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) a weekly celebration where staff and others don bolo ties in his honor. The enthusiasm has even led to a fan-made Instagram account and a petition for Will to replace the mayor's greeting at Denver International Airport.
* Despite his flamboyant style, Will is respected for his efforts to [work across party lines](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and for fostering camaraderie within the legislature, evidenced by his bipartisan breakfast burritos tradition and the widespread participation in themed Fridays celebrating him.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[COMMENTARY: How best to protect our children in school](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY GEORGE BRAUCHLER, [THE GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_In a more perfect world, we would not know the name Kendrick Castillo. In a more perfect world, the only son of John and Maria Castillo would be starting a career in robotics after graduating college, driving his jeep all over, attending church with his parents, maybe spending time with someone he met and was sweet on, and generally living the promising life of a man in his early 20s._
_But our world is less perfect._
_May 7, 2019 -- five years ago almost to the day -- in Ms. Harper's British Lit class just after lunch, two disgruntled students armed with four, fully loaded firearms, entered Classroom 107 of STEM Academy with a plan to murder 28 people. That evil plan failed solely because there were heroes in 107. _

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Denver announces plan to invest over $500M to revitalize downtown](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [KDVR](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Denver Mayor Mike Johnston has committed to [investing over $500 million to rejuvenate downtown Denver](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), targeting economic growth and the restoration of vibrancy affected by the pandemic.
* The area has experienced slower recovery than other parts of the city, with a [notable decline in business activity](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and foot traffic, and only a 1% increase in downtown sales and use taxes compared to a 40% citywide rise.
* The revitalization funds, sourced from the [Downtown Development Authority](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), will enhance housing, childcare, and public spaces without raising new taxes. Community input will be sought to ensure the developments meet local needs and are expected to be available by 2025.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---

clean_body: Your daily rundown of REAL news for REAL Coloradans. | | | | PRESENTED BY | | Happy Saturday, friends! This is the Mountain Minute for Saturday, May 11, 2024. - Heidi Ganahl | | | TODAY'S TOP STORIES BY BRIAN PORTER, Overdoses believed to be driven largely by fentanyl-laced prescription drugs are setting records annually. The death count once was similar to the each week, marking the third largest cause of pediatric deaths behind firearm-related injuries and motor vehicle collisions, a report by UCLA Health reads. It has now grown beyond that analogy. The drug often kills those , with the Centers for Disease Control finding 86 percent of those adolescents dying of a fentanyl overdose had not previously experienced a drug overdose, according to a report in The Free Press. Introduction of Fentanyl has been especially devastating in Colorado, where the Department of Public Health found in 2021 there were more than 900 deaths, and from 2020 to 2021 there was a 70 percent uptick in fentanyl deaths, . An effort to share personal stories of tragedy related to fentanyl use and seek solutions will hit theaters this coming week. The film "" will premiere in four select theaters from May 15-17. | Colorado legislators have relied heavily on imposing fees, like a new to fund mass transit, rather than raising taxes. The American Car Rental Association has , claiming the fee violates federal law against non-airport benefiting fees. The rental car industry supports that would restrict fee increases by requiring voter approval and linking fees directly to benefits received by those who pay them. The proposed rental car fee for Denver International Airport and increases consumer costs, with rental car fees constituting a significant portion of rental expenses. Colorado officials like Gov. Polis , while the rental car industry argues it threatens state financial stability and highlights its significant local economic contributions, including employment and tax revenue. | Colorado has become an iconic figure at the Capitol, celebrated for his distinctive style featuring wolf fur and bolo ties, and his unique approach to politics that includes challenging Denver's mayor to a quirky eating contest. The senator's popularity has a weekly celebration where staff and others don bolo ties in his honor. The enthusiasm has even led to a fan-made Instagram account and a petition for Will to replace the mayor's greeting at Denver International Airport. Despite his flamboyant style, Will is respected for his efforts to and for fostering camaraderie within the legislature, evidenced by his bipartisan breakfast burritos tradition and the widespread participation in themed Fridays celebrating him. | BY GEORGE BRAUCHLER, In a more perfect world, we would not know the name Kendrick Castillo. In a more perfect world, the only son of John and Maria Castillo would be starting a career in robotics after graduating college, driving his jeep all over, attending church with his parents, maybe spending time with someone he met and was sweet on, and generally living the promising life of a man in his early 20s. But our world is less perfect. May 7, 2019 -- five years ago almost to the day -- in Ms. Harper's British Lit class just after lunch, two disgruntled students armed with four, fully loaded firearms, entered Classroom 107 of STEM Academy with a plan to murder 28 people. That evil plan failed solely because there were heroes in 107. | Denver Mayor Mike Johnston has committed to , targeting economic growth and the restoration of vibrancy affected by the pandemic. The area has experienced slower recovery than other parts of the city, with a and foot traffic, and only a 1% increase in downtown sales and use taxes compared to a 40% citywide rise. The revitalization funds, sourced from the , will enhance housing, childcare, and public spaces without raising new taxes. Total Shocker. | &amount=25 Folks, I have hope for my Liberal friends ...jellyfish have survived for more than 165 million years without a brain, so there is hope for a few of them. Just going over the numbers so far and it's looking like it could be one of my worst months in recent memory. I'm not asking for much, but a few dollars would go a long way. | | | | | | I'm a simple man. I like a good book, taking my dogs for a Sunday stroll, and beating Chuck Schumer in any way possible. name: Biden Defense [Team UTS]
subject: pummeled
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Upset The Setup PAC
body:
Listen, our chances of defending the Senate just dropped dramatically.

I’m officially the most vulnerable woman in the Senate. Now, Republicans are zeroing in on Nevada as the one seat they need to win Senate control in November. Because if we lose Nevada, the entire Senate will flip red.

I’ve won tough races before, but this is shaping up to be the closest race I’ve ever faced. GOP megadonors like the Koch network are already pouring millions into this race to defeat me, and I can’t fight back alone. That’s why I’m reaching out for your urgent help as I face a major fundraising gap.

We just ran the numbers, and it looks like I need to raise at least $30,000 by midnight to have the resources to keep this campaign on track, pull ahead in the polls, and defend this seat – but right now, we're way short.

Please, can you pitch in $3 or more to split between me and Upset the Setup right now to help us turn this around before it's too late? Every dollar will go a long way toward making sure we win in Nevada and defend the Senate for Democrats. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/uts_email_2024_rosen?amount=3]

If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:

Thank you for doing your part to help defend Nevada and the Senate.

name: Tina Smith
subject: A personal story for you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
Hi there, I’d like to share something personal with you today because I believe there’s still stigma and a lack of understanding around mental health, and May represents Mental Health Awareness Month. **When I was a young woman in college, I had my first experience with depression.** At first, I thought I was just having a bad patch. Then, my roommate said to me, “You need to figure out how to get some help.” Thankfully, I listened. I got help. I had the same experience with depression after I became a young mom. It was a little worse that time, but I was fortunate to have access to resources and knew I was able to get help. _Not everyone has this level of access and opportunity to get the help they need — but I’m trying to change that._ Sharing my story with depression isn’t a difficult decision. It’s a story millions of Americans have lived firsthand — either themselves or somebody else they love. I’m doing what I can to break the stigma around talking about mental health. **But it’s not just important for me to talk about it — as your Senator, I’m using my position to take action to help people get the help they need.** That’s why I helped create the Bipartisan Mental Health Caucus in the U.S. Senate. I’ve also introduced legislation like the _Mental Health Services for Students Act_ and the _Pride in Mental Health Act_ , legislation to address the mental health crisis in our country. **[If you support these efforts to expand mental healthcare access to Americans across the country, please add your name today. I’m leading the charge in the Senate to take meaningful action.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/84774240/472573126/-1530936651?refcode=AU_TSM_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20240511_PM1_V1_S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMTc3ODU1Yi1jMDBmLWVmMTEtOTZmMy02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Mxy8ExMcShyngXbv7KvvMMEUgqvz3DOJcwvo3FBw_ZQ=&emci=9d111655-c00f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=2177855b-c00f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=6227932)** | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/84774241/472573127/-1530936651?refcode=AU_TSM_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20240511_PM1_V1_S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMTc3ODU1Yi1jMDBmLWVmMTEtOTZmMy02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Mxy8ExMcShyngXbv7KvvMMEUgqvz3DOJcwvo3FBw_ZQ=&emci=9d111655-c00f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=2177855b-c00f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=6227932)
---
We have a moral obligation to extend a helping hand to those who need it, those who need help but might not ask for it, and those who might not even know they need help at all.
We can and must take action to address the mental health crisis — and I look forward to sharing the progress we make on this important, personal issue.
Finally, I’ll leave you with this: If you or someone you love is suffering from a mental health issue, know that you are not alone. And I’ll be fighting to get the resources and help that people need.
— Tina
Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
---

PO Box 14362
St. Paul, MN 55114
United States

clean_body: | Hi there, I’d like to share something personal with you today because I believe there’s still stigma and a lack of understanding around mental health, and May represents Mental Health Awareness Month. When I was a young woman in college, I had my first experience with depression. At first, I thought I was just having a bad patch. Then, my roommate said to me, “You need to figure out how to get some help.” Thankfully, I listened. I got help. I had the same experience with depression after I became a young mom. It was a little worse that time, but I was fortunate to have access to resources and knew I was able to get help. Not everyone has this level of access and opportunity to get the help they need — but I’m trying to change that. Sharing my story with depression isn’t a difficult decision. It’s a story millions of Americans have lived firsthand — either themselves or somebody else they love. I’m doing what I can to break the stigma around talking about mental health. But it’s not just important for me to talk about it — as your Senator, I’m using my position to take action to help people get the help they need. That’s why I helped create the Bipartisan Mental Health Caucus in the U.S. Senate. I’ve also introduced legislation like the Mental Health Services for Students Act and the Pride in Mental Health Act , legislation to address the mental health crisis in our country. | We have a moral obligation to extend a helping hand to those who need it, those who need help but might not ask for it, and those who might not even know they need help at all. We can and must take action to address the mental health crisis — and I look forward to sharing the progress we make on this important, personal issue. Finally, I’ll leave you with this: If you or someone you love is suffering from a mental health issue, know that you are not alone. And I’ll be fighting to get the resources and help that people need. — Tina Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota Tina Smith for Minnesota PO Box 14362 St. Paul, MN 55114 United States | | Contributions or gifts to Tina Smith for Minnesota are not tax-deductible.

name: DC Daily Journal
subject: Never eat almonds, spinach and peanut butter (Here's why)
body:
Untitled Document

Please read the
following message from our sponsor.

If you've been led to believe
that peanut butter, almonds and spinach are good for you, today's
email might shock you and is important to read, because these 3
healthy foods could be contributing to health problems in your
body.

Let's take a quick look at why...

1. Peanut butter

While peanut butter does contain
a small amount of protein and vitamins/minerals, the cons
outweigh the pros on this food.

First of all, peanuts contain one
of the highest concentrations of aflatoxin, which is a mold toxin
that is created in peanuts due to storage in moist conditions.

Peanut butter is also very high
in inflammatory omega-6 fats and due to the processing of peanut
butter, those omega 6 fats are in a harmful oxidized state.

Most people already get too much
omega 6 fats from vegetable oils (soy, corn, canola, etc), and
this contributes to insulin resistance, heart disease, brain
inflammation, cancer, and lots more health problems, since most
people are currently eating 5-10x more omega-6 fats than we
should be eating in a healthy diet.

Lastly, peanut butter also
contains medium amounts of oxalates, which are plant-based
antinutrients that when consumed in excess can contribute to
kidney stones, thyroid and gut problems, joint problems and more.

We'll talk more about oxalates in
the next food...

2. Spinach

Spinach is promoted as a healthy
green, but contains FAR more downsides than good sides. Spinach
is one of the highest oxalate foods that's commonly eaten by
modern humans (in addition to beet greens, beets, almonds, and
other veggies and nuts).

The amount of oxalates in a
serving of spinach is extremely high, up to 600 mg in a normal
sized bowl.

The "safe" amount of oxalates in
a daily diet according to world renowned oxalate expert Sally
Norton should be between 100-200 mg per day total to avoid a lot
of the health problems caused by high oxalate plants.

So, you can see how concerning
spinach is with the extremely high oxalate levels.

As mentioned above, eating too
much oxalates too frequently can cause serious issues such as
kidney stones, thyroid problems, gut damage, skin issues, and
joint pain.

In fact, some people have even
gotten "oxalate poisoning" by eating green smoothies too often
and had to go to the ER for kidney failure.

3. Almonds

Although almonds and almond milk
are marketed as healthy, they also contain many downsides,
including all of the negatives mentioned above about too much
omega 6 fats and too much oxalates.

They're probably ok in your diet
occasionally, but I personally avoid eating almonds too often
because of these issues with excess oxalates and omega 6 fats.

Now let's take a look at a fun
little quiz below and a few more foods...

Bananas vs oranges vs apples...
(the #1 WORST fruit for your gut & energy)
According to the best-selling
author and world-renowned heart surgeon, Dr. Steven Gundry,
there's one fruit people over 40 should never eat.

This fruit can damage your gut
and disrupt your hormones. Both sapping you of energy and forcing
your body to create "sticky" fat cells that can NOT be "burned
off" with normal diet and exercise.

Do you know the fruit I'm talking
about? Make sure you do by clicking the one below you think it
is, and get the answer from Dr. Gundry himself on the next page:

>> Bananas

>> Oranges

>> Apples

>> Grapes

DCDailyJournal.com

525-K East Market Street # 114

City: Leesburg
State: Virginia
Zip Code: 20176

© Copyright DCDailyJournal.com All Rights
Reserved.

You were added to the system July 13, 2023.

with "Unsubscribe" as the subject line.

name: Jon Tester
subject: Mitch McConnell is watching like a hawk
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BOB CASEY FOR SENATE INC
body:
Hey Peter, it’s Jon Tester. [**I need you to split a donation between my campaign and Bob Casey's before our most important FEC deadline yet.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20240511-1?refcode=em20240511-1&refcode2=2942_552475_ziSt-j&akid=2942%2E552475%2EziSt-j) We’re approaching a critical turning point in this race. Let me explain.
Our final FEC deadline before the primary is on May 15th. That’s only 4 days away, and it’s the last time our numbers will go public before Montana’s general election officially kicks off.
Here’s why that matters: Mitch McConnell will be looking at our report when he decides where to spend his multi-million dollar war chest. If he thinks we’re weak, you can bet he’ll flood the airwaves with even more attacks during the most pivotal moment of our campaign so far.
But if our numbers are strong, we’ll show them that this Senate seat can’t be bought and that we’ve got a grassroots team that’ll fight them every step of the way.
**With polls tightening, we cannot afford to fall short here. That’s why I’m asking you to dig deep and help us hit our ambitious $500,000 fundraising goal.**
[**Will you split a donation between my campaign and Bob Casey's to help us reach our most important FEC goal yet and report the strongest numbers possible? I wouldn’t ask if it wasn’t necessary.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20240511-1?refcode=em20240511-1&refcode2=2942_552475_ziSt-j&akid=2942%2E552475%2EziSt-j)
**[Donate Now ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20240511-1?refcode=em20240511-1&refcode2=2942_552475_ziSt-j&akid=2942%2E552475%2EziSt-j)**
Thanks,
Jon Tester

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://go.bobcasey.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=2&akid=2942%2E552475%2EziSt-j)
* [Update Your Information](https://go.bobcasey.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://go.bobcasey.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=2942%2E552475%2EziSt-j)

To give by check:
Bob Casey for Senate
PO Box 58746
Philadelphia, PA 19102
Email us: [info@bobcasey.com](mailto:info@bobcasey.com)
PAID FOR BY BOB CASEY FOR SENATE INC.

name: Brit Eames from Jane PAC
subject: Keeping up with Jane PAC 🌍
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
Follow us on social media for the latest updates.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775168/472576655/1328794144?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105)
---
👋 Hey there! I'm Brit Eames ['Aymz'] (my Nana used to say _‘don’t let ‘em mispronounce it!’_), I'm the new Director of Social Media for the Jane Fonda Climate PAC, and I'm so excited to be on the internet with you! [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775169/472576656/226724347?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105) **My job is to make sure our followers know what’s-what in the climate fight.** Sometimes that means making a **[meme](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775171/472576657/226724347?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105)** that threads climate into contemporary culture, other times that means **[breaking down](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775172/472576658/1007083608?hl=en&img_index=1&refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105)** a little-known local election and its broader impact on our world. Just think of me as your chronically online guide to all things climate crisis _and how to fight back._ As a climate activist, you know that every election counts in the fight for our future. But like Jane says, 2024 will decide whether we _have_ a future. **That’s why I'm reaching out to make sure you’re following Jane PAC on social media — we need you in this movement and getting plugged in is _critical._** [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775173/472576659/461715929?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105) **Jane PAC on social in a 🥜 (nutshell):** ✅ The latest climate news ✅ Climate advocacy opportunities ✅ Calls for volunteer actions ✅ Voter information (if you’re in states where we’ve endorsed climate champions!) ✅ Exclusive Jane content (which is the most fun to make, btw) **Follow us on your favorite platform:** 🐦 **[Follow Jane PAC on X (Twitter)](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775174/472576660/-1227372273?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105)**
📘 **[Follow Jane PAC on Facebook](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775175/472576662/692937357?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105)**
📸 **[Follow Jane PAC on Instagram](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775176/472576663/461715929?hl=en&refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105)** [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775177/472576664/461715929?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105) My other grandmother — the one who wasn’t as worried about pronunciation — used to ask me what I wanted to do with my one precious life. _And you know what?_ Being in this fight with you is _exactly_ how I want to spend this one wild and precious life. I’ll see you out there, Brit Eames
Director of Social Media, The Jane Fonda Climate PAC [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775179/472576665/1328794144?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=&emci=cb12bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=4823105)
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775180/472576666/2105508926?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=) | [**DONATE TODAY**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775181/472576667/2105508926?refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=)
---

[**DONATE MONTHLY**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84775182/472576669/2105508926?recurring=true&refcode=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.d212bd4f-c20f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyMTJiZDRmLWMyMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hsMyzpX0SwgGhuC8_w03T4wTuAXsb-Q6ubFcV3AqS_M=)

clean_body: Follow us on social media for the latest updates. | 👋 Hey there! I'm Brit Eames ['Aymz'] (my Nana used to say ‘don’t let ‘em mispronounce it!’), I'm the new Director of Social Media for the Jane Fonda Climate PAC, and I'm so excited to be on the internet with you! My job is to make sure our followers know what’s-what in the climate fight. Sometimes that means making a that threads climate into contemporary culture, other times that means a little-known local election and its broader impact on our world. Just think of me as your chronically online guide to all things climate crisis and how to fight back. As a climate activist, you know that every election counts in the fight for our future. But like Jane says, 2024 will decide whether we have a future. That’s why I'm reaching out to make sure you’re following Jane PAC on social media — we need you in this movement and getting plugged in is critical. Jane PAC on social in a 🥜 (nutshell): ✅ The latest climate news ✅ Climate advocacy opportunities ✅ Calls for volunteer actions ✅ Voter information (if you’re in states where we’ve endorsed climate champions!) ✅ Exclusive Jane content (which is the most fun to make, btw) Follow us on your favorite platform: 🐦 📘 📸 My other grandmother — the one who wasn’t as worried about pronunciation — used to ask me what I wanted to do with my one precious life. And you know what? Being in this fight with you is exactly how I want to spend this one wild and precious life. I’ll see you out there, Brit Eames Director of Social Media, The Jane Fonda Climate PAC | = | = = Follow along: | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

body:
| I have had the pleasure of knowing Executive Councilor Cinde Warmington for decades, and I am proud to be supporting her campaign for governor. She is the only candidate in this race with the backbone to beat Kelly Ayotte this fall.

**[At the end of this message, I’m going to ask you to make a contribution to Cinde’s campaign.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/84775406/472577409/-50040771?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_CW_20240511_FR_surrogate_0_3MO_1_HonPaulHodes_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNGQ1ZTNlZi1jMjBmLWVmMTEtOTZmMy02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tBqz8GssPRnIr0jPM8UScrLzJHet2ixRhx_cV5JMwPk=)** But first, I want to explain why Cinde Warmington will make a great governor.

We need a real fighter to win against Ayotte in November and, having worked with Cinde for years, I know she’s made of tough stuff. Cinde fought back against Sununu’s dangerous abortion ban, and is a champion for reproductive rights.

Kelly Ayotte, on the other hand, led the charge in the Senate for a national abortion ban, called for the overturning of _Roe v. Wade_ , and helped Donald Trump stack the Supreme Court to make it happen.

name: Brandon Herrera
subject: Want to fire my AK-50?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Brandon Herrera Victory Committee
body:
Dear Friend,
I hope you saw the video I just released--after years of work, I've finally finished my AK-50. And boy, she's a beauty.
I'd like you to be one of the first people outside of my staff to fire it.
The day after Election Day, we will randomly select one lucky donor, fly him or her to San Antonio with a guest, all expenses paid, and give them the chance to be one of the first to fire the AK-50.
Every $5 you donate to support my campaign will earn you one entry, $50 will get you 10 entries, etc. Enter today at [https://BrandonHerreraforCongress.com/Donate](https://contact.brandonherreraforcongress.com/l/6fI53a1vdu7637OWRF387YCA/7630dESEatd3aDm763YB7630r0gQ/IFd4b2k1U6aQ12FZRFXQAQ).

My campaign for Congress is in the homestretch. Unfortunately, my treasurer says we still have a budget deficit of $198,190.32, and if we don't close that soon, all of our TV, digital, and radio ads will stop, and all of the mail and texts we need to send will stop.
We'll be dead in the water without a paddle while my opponent has unlimited money from the Swamp to attack me.
Please, help me win this election and give yourself a chance to win the opportunity to be among the first to fire this first-of-its-kind weapon by donating at [https://BrandonHerreraforCongress.com/Donate](https://contact.brandonherreraforcongress.com/l/6fI53a1vdu7637OWRF387YCA/7630dESEatd3aDm763YB7630r0gQ/IFd4b2k1U6aQ12FZRFXQAQ).
Thank you for all your support--through the long process of building this firearm and this long political campaign.
Regards,

Brandon Herrera

Paid for by the Brandon Herrera Victory Committee
[Unsubscribe here](https://contact.brandonherreraforcongress.com/unsubscribe/M3dKmjvhR8CwRJ801N0zlTlDygVorb8PbaawXpI4LLQ/GHbxLUJHhGugn7635ZCyW3rg/IFd4b2k1U6aQ12FZRFXQAQ)

Every $5 you donate to support my campaign will earn you one entry, $50 will get you 10 entries, etc. Enter today at . My campaign for Congress is in the homestretch. Unfortunately, my treasurer says we still have a budget deficit of $198,190.32, and if we don't close that soon, all of our TV, digital, and radio ads will stop, and all of the mail and texts we need to send will stop. We'll be dead in the water without a paddle while my opponent has unlimited money from the Swamp to attack me. Please, help me win this election and give yourself a chance to win the opportunity to be among the first to fire this first-of-its-kind weapon by donating at . Thank you for all your support--through the long process of building this firearm and this long political campaign. Regards, Brandon Herrera Paid for by the Brandon Herrera Victory Committee

name: Jill Stein 2024
subject: In just a few hours...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jill Stein for President 2024
body:
Join us tonight for a special livestream

| | | [ ](https://www.jillstein2024.com/?e=e813143e1dc0d3b0ffcb007ffe357333&utm_source=jillsteinforpresident&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=birthday_reminder&n=1)
---
Peter,

We'd like to invite you to an event tonight at 8:30/5:30 PM EST/PST to mark Jill's upcoming birthday and participate in a livestream to help stop genocide in Gaza -- along with some very special guests. Jill will be joined by anti-war powerhouse Medea Benjamin, Palestinian-American activist Jenan Matari, Busboys & Poets founder Andy Shallal, Columbia University Professor Anthony Zenkus, and other surprise guests. Join us to ensure the moral imperative of our era is front and center in the election by getting our campaign on the ballot in every state across the nation - especially in New York, where Democrats have created the most difficult rules in the country to keep us off the ballot. **So if you haven't already, RSVP on our site to receive a Zoom link to join tonight's event:**

The Jill Stein 2024 team

Thank you for all you do to support Jill as she leads the charge for people, planet, and peace! name: Team Baldwin
subject: Hovde’s dark money allies were caught lying about Tammy’s record and had to take down their attack ad.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
| A dark money group was caught lying on the air about Tammy’s record.
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=2594%2E35263%2EZs2Pc6)
**🚨 BREAKING: A dark money group supporting Eric Hovde was caught lying on the air about Tammy’s record 🚨** Last week, Restoration PAC — a right-wing dark money group supporting Eric Hovde — launched a $3 million ad buy attacking Tammy and blatantly lying about her record. **Now, cable providers across Wisconsin have forced Hovde’s allies to take down their egregious attack ad after it was proven false.** This kind of thing doesn’t happen very often, Peter. But it speaks volumes about how low Tammy’s Republican opponent and his dark money allies are willing to go to flip this Senate seat red. name: Jahana Hayes
subject: House Republicans just put Big Oil and Gas over people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/84777010/472583602/-1295403277?refcode=AU_JH_FR_GEN_RES_AC90_20240511_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMGYxMjBhYS1jODBmLWVmMTEtOTZmMy02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HF_Ecjk-IDMz2Y5SQvjSmZavA-AVnO1aClfeiIWDQn0=&emci=8e7660b0-ff0e-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&emdi=b0f120aa-c80f-ef11-96f3-6045bdd9e096&ceid=15529135) | Friend — About 72 hours ago, House Republicans narrowly passed H.R. 2925 — putting the profits of an industry worth several hundred billion dollars ahead of American taxpayers. In another giveaway to Big Oil and Gas corporations, the GOP voted to undermine scientific evidence and harm our public lands by allowing mining companies to hold public lands hostage for mining activities. If their bill were to become law, bad actors could block renewable energy development, conservation, and even recreational use of public lands. **I was proud to vote no, but this time, our votes were not enough.** [**We need to defend this seat in CT-05 so that we can retake the House majority. Folks, if you have just a few spare minutes today, I’m hoping you’ll let me share something important: I’m running for re-election because I believe in putting workers and families, our public lands and waters, and our individual freedoms first. My parents, a union lineman and a factory worker, inspired me to make sure everyone who works hard gets a fair shot at success. But Republicans would rather buy elections and recruit multi-millionaire candidates to challenge Democrats in key Senate seats, including here in New Mexico. **We can defeat my hedge fund executive opponent and hold the Senate Majority, but it will take a strong foundation of grassroots support. That’s why it’s so important we meet our fundraising goal before our upcoming pre-primary deadline. Shortly after, we’ll report how much we’ve raised since April and demonstrate our momentum in this race.** **[Before my campaign’s pre-primary deadline, can you chip in $10 or more to help prove the strength of a campaign that puts New Mexico values first?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/84777136/472583771/2144451455?amount=10&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20240511_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYmYxMjBhYS1jODBmLWVmMTEtOTZmMy02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N0I5re1UdjxzEYfQjyYyI8mi5sRMbU4RIHRduL82cts=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/84777139/472583775/2144451455?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20240511_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYmYxMjBhYS1jODBmLWVmMTEtOTZmMy02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N0I5re1UdjxzEYfQjyYyI8mi5sRMbU4RIHRduL82cts=)

clean_body: Folks, if you have just a few spare minutes today, I’m hoping you’ll let me share something important: I’m running for re-election because I believe in putting workers and families, our public lands and waters, and our individual freedoms first. My parents, a union lineman and a factory worker, inspired me to make sure everyone who works hard gets a fair shot at success. But Republicans would rather buy elections and recruit multi-millionaire candidates to challenge Democrats in key Senate seats, including here in New Mexico. We can defeat my hedge fund executive opponent and hold the Senate Majority, but it will take a strong foundation of grassroots support. That’s why it’s so important we meet our fundraising goal before our upcoming pre-primary deadline. Shortly after, we’ll report how much we’ve raised since April and demonstrate our momentum in this race. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you in advance for anything you can give, Martin | Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate Senator Martin Heinrich is committed to helping New Mexico become a leader in defense, tech, and energy with opportunities in every corner. **[SHOW YOUR APPRECIATION FOR OUR SOLDIERS HOLDING THE LINE! >>](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=6e68964c175d4d858eda05b99cd49225&_e=l48iw0PqkpIEaZqDI7O96gy2IYZSdyjqxNP3ozzylA-SkNoooow_t3Bao_FxBlaamRsCxOsfOSOyOuHkRxVLgG-6Mm_ImQ-8ffgou02ha_h2ZPCMI1X4AIC5HerdIdstK9AdaaIUxAJN4OGkCLhUjAECHWJeYJuxJCZ40mrwlDvJFw2dXA0i3UyWOMH3Y1_wYr4UxHGuE7kx6zALhiw-V4fTOcEFBZBFlBcwKg1BP4oz_IGs4XtEBkonSXlbak0Wmjkt0Q2OKs4mSKj20Gwqthj2s_HtgDr-XOssFhNf-rlViTwulUQVEK8k6KMREXsMsUTHFefnnUUtxlT17SePa_X4PPzrtvteRaw5YzXCbGQ32ZNXPTYVGQro09X8L6lWokIfaYQgI1zjrwrFchMGiw%3D%3D)**
Friend,

Unfortunately, every state is a border state due to Joe Biden's dangerous open border policies. That's why states across the nation are joining Texas to do the job our President refuses to do - _**SECURE OUR BORDER!**_

States like Arkansas, Indiana, and Georgia, to name a few, are sending reinforcements to help our Texas National Guard soldiers and Department of Public Safety troopers hold the line. _Texas is ground zero of Biden 's border crisis, and we are so grateful for their support. _

Teaming up with fellow Governors, our soldiers and troopers continue to redouble and reinforce our border security efforts along the southern border to deter and repel illegal immigrants and stop the smuggling of people, weapons, and deadly drugs like fentanyl into our country.

Day in and day out, these brave men and women willingly put themselves in harm's way to keep America safe, and Governor Greg Abbott wants to show them some much-deserved gratitude and appreciation for their service. _Will you help him do that?_

**We 're calling on supporters from across the country to take action before midnight tonight and join Governor Abbott in THANKING OUR SOLDIERS at the border!** **[Please follow this link to add your name and show your appreciation.>>](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=6e68964c175d4d858eda05b99cd49225&_e=l48iw0PqkpIEaZqDI7O96gy2IYZSdyjqxNP3ozzylA-SkNoooow_t3Bao_FxBlaamRsCxOsfOSOyOuHkRxVLgG-6Mm_ImQ-8ffgou02ha_h2ZPCMI1X4AIC5HerdIdstK9AdaaIUxAJN4OGkCLhUjAECHWJeYJuxJCZ40mrwlDvJFw2dXA0i3UyWOMH3Y1_wYr4UxHGuE7kx6zALhiw-V4fTOcEFBZBFlBcwKg1BP4oz_IGs4XtEBkonSXlbak0WyWPnwvlckca2Gcmd7SlxIZsV1roz_ea3skoC5Fqdys8F4fcnhqSueOCZ4Xhb51BE7bl37Z1zE5W5Bz0AnsvZ7u1LeBnTWT5l0sBgfh17Uys80keWCQoLq_hYRp3ktLJEM9_3HxWsVwt8-7SBIQsVvg%3D%3D)**

The reality is, the success of **Operation Lone Star** wouldn't be possible without our brave service members deployed at our southern border.
[THANK OUR TROOPS TODAY ->](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=6e68964c175d4d858eda05b99cd49225&_e=l48iw0PqkpIEaZqDI7O96gy2IYZSdyjqxNP3ozzylA-SkNoooow_t3Bao_FxBlaamRsCxOsfOSOyOuHkRxVLgG-6Mm_ImQ-8ffgou02ha_h2ZPCMI1X4AIC5HerdIdstK9AdaaIUxAJN4OGkCLhUjAECHWJeYJuxJCZ40mrwlDvJFw2dXA0i3UyWOMH3Y1_wYr4UxHGuE7kx6zALhiw-V4fTOcEFBZBFlBcwKg1BP4oz_IGs4XtEBkonSXlbak0W8IP3Ss87p5cACQizWA2ma4H_z5YDkyIx7mFQqd_vFQPX-7_GlHP5saNhwi-Tc6M0QzEFu_5is-_hfqO-07WQL2EhNaL_j8HkYN6obvtYf1R1Nk83X2n0LYrvBmace565oEoInYdycAEtn6x5TlTsrg%3D%3D)
Texas, with the help of states across the nation, will continue to fill the dangerous gaps created by the Biden Administration's open border policies, and we will not stop until our border is secure!

_**[More than 15,000 of you have signed your name to thank the troops, help us collect at least 5,000 MORE before the day ends when you add your name, too, Peter!](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=6e68964c175d4d858eda05b99cd49225&_e=l48iw0PqkpIEaZqDI7O96gy2IYZSdyjqxNP3ozzylA-SkNoooow_t3Bao_FxBlaamRsCxOsfOSOyOuHkRxVLgG-6Mm_ImQ-8ffgou02ha_h2ZPCMI1X4AIC5HerdIdstK9AdaaIUxAJN4OGkCLhUjAECHWJeYJuxJCZ40mrwlDvJFw2dXA0i3UyWOMH3Y1_wYr4UxHGuE7kx6zALhiw-V4fTOcEFBZBFlBcwKg1BP4oz_IGs4XtEBkonSXlbak0W-_pGQbGyMFERpHekBN-_GXAH67eZbEgyybC1_C4x4TTAUeHm5d6MgbW7anc2-R-l44tGofutWS-KZ2ifC8rIaw12Fml4tfjPC7JASv5sacIPb0WV3XFbmOHp4HvNKq97oj6fxXylMXMOxgPHAN3q1g%3D%3D)**_

Team Abbott

In Missouri, we’re all too familiar with Big Brother politicians trying to control us. That’s why people in this state have banded together to take back power at the ballot box — raising the minimum wage, legalizing weed, expanding Medicaid, and repealing an anti-union 'Right-to-Work' law, all by huge margins since 2018.

Now in Josh Hawley’s first election since leading the charge to overturn Roe — which made Missouri the first state in the nation to enact an extreme no-exception abortion ban — he’s going to see what voters think about Big Brother politicians. **And if recent election wins for abortion rights in red states like Kansas, Kentucky, and Ohio have proven anything, it’s that Josh Hawley should be afraid.**

This year, we’ll have a chance to end Missouri’s extreme abortion ban and hold Josh Hawley accountable for making it happen. But we need your help to get it done.

[So Peter, will you help us keep up this momentum by splitting a donation of any amount — even just $5 — between my grassroots campaign today and Defeat Republicans today? I’m counting on real people like you to power this movement forward, and I’m not taking a cent from corporate PACs, so I mean it when I say anything makes a big impact. ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/84778095/472587306/1360213794?refcode=DR_SPLIT_FR_90DO_240511_1_2&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNi8xLzg1ODM1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM4ZjM4OTc1LWNiMGYtZWYxMS05NmYzLTYwNDViZGQ5ZTA5NiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=0vaj5PadUApgBO1fyVfVzIBHVddO93o6H9kHtIBu_Xw=)

But we can’t take our foot off the gas now – the stakes are too high. Electing Democratic leaders who will support President Biden’s agenda in statehouses across the country is critical to building a better future in your state. But it’s up to grassroots supporters like you to own a piece of this fight and give state Democrats the resources they need to go toe-to-toe against their Republican opponents.
